Summary: Up to US$25,000 per project
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: CSOs and independent media legally registered in Ukraine (or relocated to neighbouring countries) and currently operating in Ukraine or neighbouring states.
Eligibility: Ukrainian CSOs, independent media, civic-tech, youth orgs, and recovery-focused local initiatives
Geographic Focus: Ukraine (including relocated orgs in neighbouring countries)
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Collaboration with local partners encouraged but not required
End Summary: Grants to support urgent and long-term civic, media and recovery needs in Ukraine amid ongoing war-related disruptions.

Sponsoring Organization/Funder: Ida B. Wells Society for Investigative Reporting
Funding/Salary/Benefit: A free, Atlanta-based fellowship for U.S.-based journalists to receive in-depth training in investigative reporting. Includes monthly sessions and support to produce a publishable investigative story.
Summary: Fellowship is free; travel and lodging covered
Application Deadline: Fellowship is free; travel and lodging covered
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: U.S.-based journalists with at least 3 years of professional experience; freelancers must have newsroom commitment to publish project
Eligibility: Journalists and reporters transitioning to or growing in investigative reporting
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Individual applicants only; must have support from newsroom or publication partner
End Summary: A strong U.S.-based fellowship for journalists seeking to develop and publish investigative stories with expert guidance and in-person training.

Funding/Salary/Benefit: Internship for candidates interested in journalism and digital media. Responsibilities include content creation, social media strategy, video editing, and supporting regional events and communication workshops.
Summary: Unspecified stipend
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Bachelor’s or Master’s students, or early-career professionals with journalism/media or communications skills; design/video editing preferred.
End Summary: Closely aligned to media and communications, with elements of design and digital production but not explicitly product/service design focused.

Opportunity Name: The Ferriss – UC Berkeley Psychedelic Journalism Fellowship (2026)
Sponsoring Organization/Funder: UC Berkeley Graduate School of Journalism & UC Berkeley Center for the Science of Psychedelics
Funding/Salary/Benefit: A reporting fellowship issuing ten $10,000 grants to journalists to support in-depth print and audio stories on the science, policy, business, and culture aspects of psychedelics, nurturing a new generation of coverage in this field.
Summary: $10,000 reporting grant per recipient; intended to underwrite reporting costs and living expenses.
Application Deadline: $10,000 reporting grant per recipient; intended to underwrite reporting costs and living expenses.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Professional journalists or experienced journalists with a strong track record; applicants should propose compelling, underreported story projects in print or audio illustrating significant psychedelic-related topics. Open to all nationalities; submissions require pitch, work samples, resume, and references.
Eligibility: Journalists (print/audio), independent reporters, freelance journalists covering science, policy, culture, and business beats.
Geographic Focus: Global (open internationally; awards administered by UC Berkeley in the U.S.)
Consortium/Partnership Notes: No consortium requirement; individual fellowships only. Co-application or team submissions not allowed.
End Summary: A competitive global fellowship granting $10,000 to journalists to report deep, narrative stories on psychedelic research, policy, business, and cultural issues.

Funding/Salary/Benefit: A part-time 6-month open-source intelligence (OSINT) fellowship contributing to investigative work, focused on expanding journalism in underrepresented communities.
Summary: €1,100/month stipend; 5 days leave for the duration; remote work.
Application Deadline: €1,100/month stipend; 5 days leave for the duration; remote work.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Early-career journalists or journalism-adjacent professionals from Majority World countries with OSINT and social media research skills.
Geographic Focus: Global (remote; targeted to Majority World citizens)
End Summary: Fellowship supports OSINT-focused journalists from underrepresented communities.

Funding/Salary/Benefit: A part-time 6-month reporting fellowship embedded within high-impact investigative projects; fellows work with Lighthouse’s global distributed newsroom and are mentored by senior journalists.
Summary: €1,100/month stipend; 5 days leave for the duration; remote work.
Application Deadline: €1,100/month stipend; 5 days leave for the duration; remote work.
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Early-career professionals with less than 5 years of experience in journalism or related fields; strong research and writing skills required.
Geographic Focus: Global (remote; based anywhere with good internet access)
End Summary: 6-month part-time fellowship supporting development of cross-border investigative journalism skills.

Opportunity Name: CIJI Grants Towards Journalism Trust Initiative Certification (2026)
Sponsoring Organization/Funder: Collaborative and Investigative Journalism Initiative (CIJI), coordinated by Tactical Tech, with RSF and other partners
Funding/Salary/Benefit: Grants to support media organizations in the European Union to achieve Journalism Trust Initiative (JTI) certification, promoting ethical, transparent, and accountable journalism.
Summary: €1,500 to €5,000 per grantee based on audit cost and quote from certification body
Application Deadline: €1,500 to €5,000 per grantee based on audit cost and quote from certification body
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Legally registered media organizations (print, TV, radio, or digital) operating in any of the 27 EU Member States; must complete JTI self-assessment and publish Transparency Report
Eligibility: Media organizations aiming for JTI certification
Consortium/Partnership Notes: No consortium requirement; individual media organizations apply independently
End Summary: Supports EU-based media outlets in obtaining JTI certification to strengthen transparency and editorial standards.

Sponsoring Organization/Funder: Shorenstein Center on Media, Politics and Public Policy at Harvard Kennedy School (Funded by the Goldsmith Fund of the Greenfield Foundation)
Funding/Salary/Benefit: Annual award recognizing investigative journalism that promotes more effective and ethical conduct of government, public policy, or politics in the U.S.
Summary: Winner: $25,000 USD; Finalists: $10,000 USD each; Prize money goes to journalists directly
Application Deadline: Winner: $25,000 USD; Finalists: $10,000 USD each; Prize money goes to journalists directly
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Open to U.S. news media including newspapers, magazines, broadcast, radio, podcasts, online outlets; documentaries publicly released in U.S.; work must be published between Jan 1–Dec 31, 2025; books not eligible
Eligibility: Journalists, media organizations, investigative teams, documentary filmmakers
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Collaborations allowed; lead news organization must submit and list partners; secondary collaborators don’t count toward entry limits
End Summary: Prestigious annual U.S. award for impactful investigative reporting; submissions open until Jan 5, 2026

Opportunity Name: Global Media Defence Fund (GMDF) – Call for Partnerships 2025
Funding/Salary/Benefit: Call for proposals supporting projects that bolster journalists’ legal safety and media freedom through investigative journalism, strategic litigation, and legal assistance.
Summary: Total budget of $800,000; individual grants between $15,000–$50,000; approx. 16–25 grants available
Application Deadline: Total budget of $800,000; individual grants between $15,000–$50,000; approx. 16–25 grants available
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Non-profit organizations working on media freedom, legal protection of journalists, strategic litigation, or investigative journalism; proposal with required documents must be submitted
Eligibility: Non-profit organizations focused on journalism, legal rights, and media freedom
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Collaborations encouraged; proposals may be submitted jointly by relevant entities
End Summary: Funding call supporting global initiatives to enhance legal protection and media freedom for journalists

Sponsoring Organization/Funder: Ripples Centre for Data and Investigative Journalism (RCDIJ)
Funding/Salary/Benefit: Supports small to mid-scale investigative and data journalism projects on public interest issues such as health, education, environment, corruption, technology, etc.

Application Deadline: Up to $10,000 per project; includes editorial support and publication via Ripples Nigeria
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Individual journalists based in Africa (freelance or employed); open to all media formats; application must include project plan, budget, past work, CV, and ID

End Summary: Rolling fund offering grants and editorial backing for investigative journalism in Africa

Opportunity Name: Dr Goodluck Jonathan Freedom of Information Awards 2026
Funding/Salary/Benefit: Annual awards recognizing excellence in journalistic use and promotion of the Freedom of Information Act, 2011 in Nigeria. Two categories: Most Active User and Most Outstanding Promoter of the FOI Act.
Summary: Plaque, certificate, and an unspecified prize for each winner
Application Deadline: Plaque, certificate, and an unspecified prize for each winner
Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Nigerian journalists across print, broadcast, online, or multimedia; must show evidence of FOI use/promotion; no record of professional misconduct
Eligibility: Journalists promoting transparency and accountability through FOI Act in Nigeria
Consortium/Partnership Notes: Individual applications only; collaborations not specified
End Summary: Awards presented on May 28, 2026, in Abuja, marking the 15th anniversary of the FOI Act

Funding/Salary/Benefit: Five six-month fellowships placing early-career Black Canadian journalists in major newsrooms to amplify Black voices and improve coverage of Black issues
Summary: $35,000 stipend over six months; includes mentoring, training, and newsroom experience

Consortium/Partnership Notes: Individual fellowship; applicants must submit proposal, CV, work samples, and reference
End Summary: Supports Indigenous journalists to report on significant issues and gain newsroom experience with CBC Indigenous

Funding/Salary/Benefit: Provides funding for in-depth, underreported global stories across any topic or medium, encouraging ambitious investigative journalism worldwide.

Application Deadline: Project-based funding; amount varies depending on scope.

Opportunity Name: Knight‑Bagehot Fellowship in Economics and Business Journalism

Consortium/Partnership Notes: Administered by Earth Journalism Network under Internews Asia-Pacific project, funded by Sida

Sponsoring Organization/Funder: Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism, University of Oxford

Target Sector/Beneficiaries: Early-career journalists from low- to upper-middle-income tropical countries (based on World Bank classification); no formal education required; must be fluent in English for this cohort; must commit ~10 hours/week remotely
Geographic Focus: Tropical biodiversity hotspot countries; that is low- to upper-middle-income tropical country, as classified by the World Bank (excluding high-income tropical countries). A list of eligible countries can be found on the website.
Consortium/Partnership Notes: No formal consortium; managed by Mongabay with multiple language-based cohorts
End Summary: Remote fellowship with editorial mentoring and stipend to report on environmental conservation from tropical countries

Consortium/Partnership Notes: Individual application; some may be funded via sponsor organizations
End Summary: A globally renowned fellowship offering journalists time, resources, and mentorship to explore key journalism challenges at Oxford.

Opportunity Name: O’Brien Fellowship in Public Service Journalism (2025‑26)
Sponsoring Organization/Funder: Marquette University — Diederich College of Communication

- Moving allowance up to $7,000
- Travel & research allowance up to $9,000
- Health insurance option for independents
- Employee benefits continue if fellow is employed

- $75,000 stipend for 9 months
- Residency allowance up to $12,000
- Moving allowance up to $7,000
- Travel & research allowance up to $9,000
- Health insurance option for independents
- Employee benefits continue if fellow is employed
Target Sector/Beneficiaries:
- Staff-employed or freelance journalists residing in the U.S. or U.S. territories
- At least 5 years of professional journalism experience
- Open to journalists across media formats (print, broadcast, digital, podcasts, etc.)
- No academic prerequisites
Consortium/Partnership Notes: No formal consortia required; collaboration with Marquette students and faculty is part of the program
End Summary: Prestigious fellowship offering significant resources for impactful investigative reporting and student mentorship.

Opportunity Name: Open Call for Investigative Journalists: BIRN Investigative Journalists Programme 2026
Funding/Salary/Benefit: BIRN is inviting individual journalists to apply for grants to produce in‑depth investigative stories that shed light on critical social, political and economic issues. The programme includes financial support and professional mentoring to develop impactful journalism.

Eligibility: Journalists, including freelancers, working on investigative journalism

End Summary: Ongoing open call until 20 grants are awarded; includes mentoring and funding for impactful investigative stories
